Little Rich Girl Cocktail bar & lounge · 101 N. Leroux St.
Beneath Pompette
Open until late
Every drink tells a story. — Little Rich Girl
01The Premise

Timeless Classicism,
Innovative Mixology.

Sunken in the streets of downtown Flagstaff, beneath Pompette Bistro, lies Little Rich Girl — a unique cocktail bar set within a historic building that has stood for over a century. Under the visionary leadership of James Beard-nominated Rochelle Daniel and Bar Ambassador Michael Hopper, LRG is not just a bar; it’s an experience.

We celebrate the perfect blend of timeless classicism and innovative mixology. A thoughtfully curated space invites you to immerse yourself in an atmosphere where creativity meets craftsmanship.

02

Atmosphere.

Step inside and be enveloped by rich purples and deep blue velvet furnishings, complemented by the warm embrace of dark wooden panels. A long copper bar, antique mirrors, cocktails on vintage coupes — a luxurious backdrop for a menu that pushes the boundaries of flavor.

Photographs — Little Rich Girl.

03

The Space.

Designed by Mendil + Meyer of Brooklyn. The whole basement of 101 Leroux runs nearly a full block — bar & lounge to the south, a private dining room that opens or closes, plus restrooms and back-of-house. Two rooms, one trick door, and a copper-topped bar with 24 seats.

The Floor Plan 101 Leroux · Basement Level
  1. 1Entry & Hostess
  2. 2Bar & Dining
  3. 3Private Dining Room
  4. 4Office
  5. 5Pastry
  6. 6Restroom Hallway
  7. 7Mens
  8. 8Womens

Renderings by Mendil + Meyer Design Studio

Where every moment is crafted to perfection.
Little Rich Girl
04

Bar Book.

A short list, written tight. Classic cocktails done properly, plus a house list of seven that lean a little brash. Bar Book by The Made Shop.

“Every drink
tells a story.”

A working preview of the bar book — the full list runs ten pages, with wine, spirits, and small bites.
House cocktails by the LRG bar team. Bar book design by The Made Shop.

Creativity meets craftsmanship.
Little Rich Girl
05

Downstairs.

An unmarked door beneath Pompette. Walk down, find the bar, ask about private dining. If the room’s open, you can sit at any banquette. If it’s booked, the bartenders will get you a seat at the copper.

Reservations open the week we open. Until then, send us a note and we’ll text you when the door unlocks.

Get on the list Private Events

somewhere below the bistro.
